description: Hidden gems and trending spots from H2 2025 / 2026

description.en: 2025's best hawker carrot cake in Yishun. The crispy fragrant crust gives way to pillow-soft carrot cake inside. Served with rare cai poh on the side. Worth the hour-long wait.

spots.best_time: Morning to lunch, before queue peaks
tips: The black version (dark soy) is the crowd favorite
S$4-5 per plate — incredible value
Deep in Yishun heartland — take MRT to Yishun then bus

description.en: Named 2025's best hawker wanton mee, run by a lady who's been at this Toa Payoh stall for 30 years. Old-school noodles with Ipoh-style shredded chicken hor fun as the secret menu winner.

address.en: Toa Payoh Palm Spring Market, Toa Payoh, Singapore

tips: Order the Ipoh-style Shredded Chicken Hor Fun — it's the hidden star
Add chicken feet (S$2) for the full experience
S$4-5 for a plate — old school pricing

description.en: Geylang East's best-kept secret prata stall. Wide variety from plain (just S$1.30) to creative fillings. Authentic hawker experience in a neighborhood market locals love.

address.en: Geylang East Market & Food Centre, 1 Geylang Serai, Singapore 402001

tips: Plain prata at S$1.30 is the benchmark
The egg prata is crispy perfection
Near Paya Lebar MRT — short walk into heartland

description.en: The definition of 'hidden gem' — tucked in a sleepy eatery on Changi Road. George himself runs the stall, serving Katong-style laksa with rich coconut gravy and fresh prawns.

tips: S$6-8 per bowl — generous portions
The laksa gravy is rich with fresh coconut milk
In the Katong/Joo Chiat area — combine with cafe hopping

description.en: Opened October 2025 on Joo Chiat Road by a Parisian trained in Versailles. Classic French pastries from almond cream croissants to hazelnut praline eclairs. Blue awning marks the spot.

tips: The Almond Cream Croissant (S$6.50) is the signature
Chef-owner is ex-Tiong Bahru Bakery
Look for the bright blue awning

description.en: Weekend-only minimalist botanical matcha bar on Joo Chiat Road. Grab a cup of matcha and design your own takeaway flower tray. Three tea profiles from S$6.50.

tips: Weekend only — check opening hours
The flower arrangement activity makes it unique
Small space, focused experience — not a work cafe

description.en: Intimate eight-seater fine dining on Joo Chiat Road. Chef Law Jia-Jun serves a seven-course prix-fixe menu (S$138++) celebrating seasonal Southeast Asian ingredients. Reservation essential.

tips: Only 8 seats — book well in advance
Seven-course menu changes with seasonal ingredients
One of the most intimate dining experiences in Singapore

description.en: Cocktail bar in Joo Chiat with stunning Antoni Gaudi-inspired architecture — curved interiors, organic shapes, and mosaic tables. Signature cocktails include The Smoking Architect.

tips: The Smoking Architect (S$26) is the must-try signature
The interior design alone is worth visiting
In the heart of Joo Chiat's new bar scene

description.en: New speakeasy on Amoy Street where bubbles reign supreme. Hidden behind a red door in an Italian sandwich deli. Velvet sofas, checkered floors, gold details, and rare grower champagnes.

tips: Enter through the Italian deli — look for the red door
Champagne Louis Roederer and rare grower labels available
Moody, intimate atmosphere perfect for special occasions

description.en: Korean cocktail dining-bar in Tanjong Pagar by the Jigger & Pony Group. Opened January 2026, anchored in Korean values of craft (Kki), warmth (Jeong), and joy (Heung).

tips: From the Jigger & Pony team — pedigree cocktail bar
Korean-inspired cocktails and dining menu
Opened January 2026 — one of Singapore's hottest new bars

description.en: Hidden behind Dirty Supper in Tiong Bahru. Zero-waste cocktail bar using kitchen scraps creatively — think fat-washed bourbon Boulevardier and pickled lemon brine martini. Low-key local vibe.

tips: Enter through Dirty Supper to find it
Zero-waste concept — all cocktail ingredients come from the kitchen
Tiong Bahru locals' favorite hidden bar

description.en: Hidden bar tucked inside a heritage coffee shop near Tiong Bahru, inspired by small grilling stalls in Osaka. By day it's a mee pok stall; at night, enter the metal door in the Moh Guan alley.

address.en: Hua Bee, 78 Moh Guan Terrace, Tiong Bahru, Singapore 162078

tips: Look for the metal door in the alley — that's the entrance
Japanese grilling concept with excellent yakitori
The heritage shophouse setting is authentically Tiong Bahru

description.en: Micro-bakery in a quiet Tiong Bahru HDB block. Owner Karis Lim handcrafts inventive small-batch bakes like Crab Rangoon Salt Bread and Tako Floss Pretzel Balls. Feels like a well-kept secret.

address.en: Kim Tian Road, Tiong Bahru, Singapore

tips: Small-batch means limited stock — arrive early
The Crab Rangoon Salt Bread (S$5.40) is the standout
Deep in HDB territory — truly off the tourist path

description.en: Opened March 2026 near Tiong Bahru MRT by the Glass Roasters team. Emphasizes quality sourcing for both coffee and tea, with craft blends and ceremonial grade matcha lattes.

address.en: 61 Seng Poh Lane, Tiong Bahru, Singapore 160061

tips: From the Glass Roasters team — quality coffee pedigree
Try the Maui Pure (S$10.80) craft blend
Near Tiong Bahru MRT

name.en: Thunderbolt Tea by Boon Lay Traditional Hakka Lui Cha

description.en: Most Gen Zs are unfamiliar with Hakka lei cha (thunder tea rice). This Paya Lebar stall serves humongous portions packed with vegetables for just S$4.50. A healthy hawker heritage dish.

tips: White Rice Thunder Tea at S$4.50 is massive value
Healthy option in the hawker world — all vegetables and herbs
A dying Hakka culinary tradition worth preserving

description.en: Named 2025's best hawker Middle Eastern cuisine. Tucked in Kampung Admiralty Hawker Centre, this stall serves authentic Arabic dishes beyond standard kebabs. A rare find in Singapore's hawker scene.

address.en: Kampung Admiralty Hawker Centre, 676 Woodlands Drive 71, Singapore 730676

tips: Beyond the usual kebab fare — try their rice platters
In the northern heartland — worth the MRT ride to Admiralty
Kampung Admiralty is one of Singapore's newest hawker centres

description.en: Chef Eugene Chee's playful Southeast Asian restaurant on Joo Chiat Road. Must-tries include Buah Keluak Pork Belly braised overnight and Signature Duck Fat Financiers.

tips: The Buah Keluak Pork Belly is braised overnight — order it
Duck Fat Financiers are an unexpectedly good dessert
On Joo Chiat — the world's coolest street per Time Out 2025

description.en: Family-run hawker stall in Tiong Bahru serving plates piled with lemon chicken, braised pork, and rich curry sauce for around S$5. Old-school Hainanese comfort food at its finest.

address.en: Tiong Bahru Market, 30 Seng Poh Road, Singapore 168898

tips: Go for the mixed plate with curry sauce — that's the move
Family-run for decades — genuine heritage food
In Tiong Bahru Market upstairs hawker centre

description.en: This nasi lemak stall walks the walk and talks the talk. Three branches but Bendemeer is considered the best. Fragrant coconut rice with crispy chicken and sambal that delivers on the name.

address.en: Bendemeer Market & Food Centre, 29 Bendemeer Road, Singapore 330029

tips: The Bendemeer branch is the original and best
Sambal is what sets it apart — properly spicy
Near Boon Keng MRT

description.en: Kings Cart Coffee by day transforms into an oyster and cocktail bar by night. A dual-concept hidden in plain sight on Joo Chiat — catch it after dusk for fresh oysters and creative cocktails.

tips: Coffee shop by day, oyster bar by night — visit after sunset
Fresh oysters paired with cocktails
Part of the new wave of Joo Chiat nightlife
